paper_title: Tree Core Sampling, Tree-Ring Width, and Stable Isotope Measurement

methods.description: Process for extracting tree cores and measuring tree-ring width to study growth patterns and environmental conditions.

steps.description: 23 Bosnian pine trees were sampled in the primaeval forest. One core per tree was extracted at breast height (1.3 m) using a Pressler borer.
steps.searchable_summary: extract tree cores using Pressler borer

steps.description: Cores were sampled in the direction parallel to the slope to avoid compression of the wood.
steps.searchable_summary: orient cores parallel to slope to avoid compression

steps.description: After surface preparation, core samples were measured using a VIAS TimeTable measuring system.
steps.searchable_summary: measure tree-ring width using VIAS TimeTable

steps.description: Tree-ring width was measured to an accuracy of 0.01 mm, and each series was cross-dated using PAST4.
Series were statistically controlled using the COFECHA program.

methods.description: Procedure for isolating alpha-cellulose from tree cores and analyzing stable carbon and oxygen isotopes to infer past climatic conditions.

steps.description: Four core samples were selected for isotopic analysis. Annual increments were separated and packed into Teflon filter bags.
steps.searchable_summary: separate and pack annual increments for isotopic analysis

steps.description: Alpha-cellulose was extracted using the modified Jayme-Wise isolation method.
Teflon filter bags were washed with NaOH and NaClO2 solutions, then dried and stored.
steps.searchable_summary: wash and dry samples for storage

steps.description: Samples were weighed and combusted for δ13C and δ18O isotope determination using an elemental analyzer and mass spectrometer.
steps.searchable_summary: determine isotopes using elemental analyzer and mass spectrometer

steps.description: System was calibrated using certified reference materials with known isotopic ratios.

methods.method_name: Tree-Ring Width Standardization and Correlation Analysis
methods.description: Standardization of tree-ring width data to remove non-climatic growth trends and correlation analysis with climatic data.

steps.description: TRW datasets were standardized using negative exponential curves, cubic smoothing splines, and regional curve standardization.
steps.searchable_summary: standardize TRW datasets using various methods

steps.description: TRW indices were calculated as residuals from estimated growth curves.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate TRW indices from growth curves

steps.description: Final chronologies were calculated using robust bi-weighted means.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate final chronologies using bi-weighted means

steps.description: Correlations with climatic factors were calculated using the R package 'treeclim'.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate correlations with climate data using 'treeclim'

steps.description: Temporal and spatial correlations were analyzed using CRU TS3.24.01 database.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze temporal and spatial correlations using CRU TS3.24.01
